subject: Civics
title: Role of the Government in Health

questions.text: In a democracy, what do people expect the government to work for?
questions.explanation: People expect the government to provide education, health, employment, and housing.

questions.text: Health means our ability to remain free of:
questions.explanation: Health is not just about diseases; it also includes mental well-being and a clean environment.

questions.text: Which of the following is a public health service?
options: Health centers run by the government
questions.explanation: Public health services are a chain of health centres and hospitals run by the government for all citizens.

questions.text: What is the primary reason why many people in India do not get proper healthcare?
questions.explanation: High costs of private care and the concentration of doctors in urban areas create access barriers.

questions.text: The public health system is 'public' because:
options: It is meant for all citizens and funded by taxes
It is located in public parks
questions.explanation: It is established by the government for the welfare of all citizens using public tax money.

questions.text: Which country is considered one of the healthiest in Central America?
questions.explanation: Costa Rica decided not to have an army and spent that money on health and education instead.

questions.text: Diseases that spread from one person to another through water, food, or air are:
questions.explanation: Examples include diarrhea, worms, hepatitis, etc.

questions.text: Private health facilities are generally concentrated in:
questions.explanation: Private services are profit-driven and are mostly found in large cities.

questions.text: What does PHC stand for in the Indian health system?
questions.explanation: Primary Health Centres serve groups of villages in rural areas.

questions.text: According to our Constitution, it is the primary duty of the government to ensure the welfare of the people and provide:
questions.explanation: The right to life is a fundamental right, and health is essential to protect it.
